items.name: False-9 Drop & Wing Dive
items.blurb: The nine drops between the lines while both wingers dive diagonally into the channels he vacates.
items.when_to_use: Triggered whenever the ball reaches a facing midfielder, the single most copied rotation of the modern era.
coaching_points_json: The wingers move the instant the nine drops, not after.
The dilemma is the point: following the nine opens the channel, passing him off frees a man between the lines.
items.youth_takeaway: One player dropping can create two runs somewhere else: movement is a team language, not a solo act.

steps.caption: The ball reaches the six or eight facing forward.
The nine drops between the lines to receive.
Both wingers cut diagonal runs into the channels the instant he drops.
The near eight pushes to occupy the vacated nine's space.

extras_json.trigger: Ball reaches the six or eight facing forward.
extras_json.creates: A free receiver in the nine, plus two runners attacking the channels at once.
extras_json.defenders_dilemma: The centre back must either follow the nine and open the channel behind him, or pass him off and concede a free man between the lines.

items.name: The Strikers' Scissors
items.blurb: One striker drops short while the other crosses diagonally behind him, alternating roles every sequence.
items.when_to_use: Any facing-forward receiver in midfield can trigger it, a staple of the 3-5-2's attacking shape.
coaching_points_json: The two strikers must read each other, not run a script: one drops only if the other is already crossing.
Centre backs cannot hand off marks cleanly if the movement never stops.
items.youth_takeaway: Two forwards who move off each other are harder to mark than two forwards who don't.

steps.caption: One striker drops short to offer the bounce option.
The other crosses behind him diagonally in the opposite direction.
The pair alternates roles again on the next sequence.

extras_json.creates: Constant bounce and in-behind availability from one strike partnership.
extras_json.defenders_dilemma: Centre backs must swap marks mid-action, endlessly: the whole attacking argument of the 3-5-2.

items.name: Overlapping CB
items.blurb: The outside centre back overlaps beyond his own wingback while the wingback tucks into the half-space.
items.when_to_use: Best from a back three when the wingback is pinned inside and needs an unmarked outlet.
coaching_points_json: The remaining two centre backs and the pivot must hold the rest-defence triangle behind the run.
This works because no opponent's marking scheme has an assignment for an overlapping centre back.
items.youth_takeaway: Innovation in football often means finding the player nobody thought to mark.

steps.caption: The wingback receives, pinned inside by his marker.
The outside centre back overlaps beyond his own wingback.
The wingback tucks into the half-space as the remaining defenders form the rest-defence triangle.

extras_json.trigger: The wingback receives, pinned inside by his marker.
extras_json.creates: A crosser nobody's marking scheme accounts for.
extras_json.defenders_dilemma: There is no assignment for an overlapping centre back, so the run is effectively unmarkable by design.
